title: Maria angusticornis
intro: Maria angusticornis, also known as Maria's narrow-horned orchid, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Andes mountain range. The plant is prized for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Maria angusticornis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Maria angusticornis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Maria angusticornis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. This family comprises plants commonly known as bellflowers. The Campanulaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Campanuloideae subfamily. Within this subfamily, Maria angusticornis is placed in the Campanula genus. The exact classification within the Campanula genus may require further research to confirm its precise position.
